pattern: Greedily deciding, position by position, whether the next element can extend the kept sequence avoids having to search over which elements to delete.
hints: Think of building the surviving array one element at a time: every element either starts a new pair or completes the current one.

Walk through the array keeping a running 'kept' sequence; if you're about to start a new pair, always keep the element, but if you're about to complete a pair, only keep it when it differs from the pair's first element.

If an element would complete a pair with the same value as its partner, deleting it is always at least as good as any alternative -- and if the kept sequence ends on an unfinished pair, one more deletion fixes that.

edge_inputs.description: every element identical, forcing many deletions
a single duplicate hidden in the middle
minimum value zero repeated near a beautiful boundary

stress.description: a long array of small random values at the constraint maximum length, so duplicate pairs are common
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
